title: Explain Settings
short_answer: Settings are the app's main configuration knobs.
text: In this app, settings live in app/settings and are loaded into AppConfig. Use settings to control modes, loops, LLM, TTS, audio, UI, and defaults.

title: Explain App Settings
text: app_settings.py controls app name, offline mode, system mode, background capture, tools, web search, cleanup days, and UI debug panels.

title: Explain Play Settings
short_answer: play_settings.py controls what starts when the app runs.
text: play_settings.py controls UI, assistant loop, STT loop, screenshot loop, default system mode, default persona, and play-loop behavior.

title: Explain Audio Settings
short_answer: audio_settings.py controls microphone, STT audio, and playback device settings.
text: Use audio settings when changing input device, STT capture behavior, or output playback configuration. This is important for voice input and TTS output.

title: Explain TTS Settings
text: TTS settings define the speech backend, voice, language, sample rate, and other spoken-output defaults. Use it when changing how the assistant speaks.

title: Explain LLM Settings
short_answer: llm_settings.py controls LLM provider and model behavior.
text: LLM settings define provider, model names, timeouts, max tokens, and temperature. Use it when changing Ollama or model behavior.

title: Explain AppConfig
short_answer: AppConfig is the built configuration object used by the app.
text: AppConfig combines settings from app/settings into one usable config object. The app should read final configuration from AppConfig instead of scattering defaults everywhere.

title: Explain ENV
short_answer: ENV values override or configure parts of the app without editing code.
text: ENV is useful for model provider, Ollama URL, model names, TTS backend, TTS voice, and other environment-specific settings.

title: Explain System Mode
short_answer: System Mode describes how active the assistant should be.
text: System modes include passive, listening_light, smart_background, and fully_active. In the current app, mode is stored and visible, but not every service fully obeys it yet.

title: Explain Default Persona
short_answer: Default Persona is the persona used when the app starts without another saved choice.
text: The default persona is configured in play settings. It helps decide which profile loads on startup, unless runtime state already has another active persona.
